  compile    Compile and optionally upload code to a microcontroller. Further
             options allow to debug the uploaded code.

  help       Display more information on a specific command.

result.stdout:
ArduinoCsCompiler - Version 1.2.0.0
This tool is experimental - expect many missing features and that the behavior will change.
Active runtime version .NET 8.0.25
acs 1.2.0-prerelease.26169.1+9c867ff2cf01fd8c451acae6d25950ef5aa85abc
The .NET Foundation
USAGE:
Compile and upload a program to the first attached microcontroller:
  acs compile MyApp.exe
Compile and upload a particular function (and it's dependencies) to a board on
COM3, using 115200 Baud:
  acs compile --baudrate 115200 --entrypoint MainFunction --port COM3
  MyAssembly.dll
Compile and upload a program via Network (WiFi) and start a debugging session:
  acs compile --debug --network 192.168.1.33 --verbose WeatherStation.exe

  -e, --entrypoint            (Default: Main) Entry point of program. Must be
                              the name of a static method taking no arguments or
                              a single string[] array.

  --compileonly               (Default: false) Only compile the code, don't
                              upload. Check whether the code could be used on
                              the microcontroller, but don't attempt an actual
                              upload.

  --run                       (Default: true) Run the program after uploading.
                              The compiler will switch to execution mode after
                              upload and run the program, printing any log
                              output to the console.

  -d, --debug                 Start the interactive remote debugger after the
                              program has started.

  --mapfile                   File to store the token map into.

  --keepifcurrent             (Default: false) If specified, the program will
                              not be transmitted if it is already loaded.
                              Defaults to false, because not all changes might
                              be caught.

  -c, --culture               The name of the culture to use for
                              'CultureInfo.CurrentCulture'. Must be a valid
                              culture name such as 'de-CH' or 'Invariant'.
                              Defaults to the current culture during compile.

  --preview                   (Default: false) Enable preview features of the
                              runtime

  -s, --suppress              Suppress the given class(es). Removes these
                              classes (fully qualified names) from the execution
                              set. Separate by ','

  -p, --port                  The serial port where the microcontroller is
                              connected. Defaults to auto-detect.

  -b, --baudrate              (Default: 115200) The baudrate to program the
                              microcontroller.

  -n, --network               An IP address to connect to (with optional port
                              number).

  -v, --verbose               Output verbose messages.

  -q, --quiet                 (Default: false) Minimal output only. This is
                              ignored if -v is specified

  --no-progress               (Default: false) Suppress printing progress
                              messages

  --help                      Display this help screen.

  --version                   Display version information.

  StartupAssembly (pos. 0)    Required. Input file/assembly. A dll or exe file
                              containing the startup code

Command line parsing error

ArduinoCsCompiler - Version 1.2.0.0
This tool is experimental - expect many missing features and that the behavior will change.
Active runtime version .NET 8.0.25
acs 1.2.0-prerelease.26169.1+9c867ff2cf01fd8c451acae6d25950ef5aa85abc
The .NET Foundation

  -t, --targetpath    Target path for the generated files. Defaults to the
                      Arduino workspace directory

  --FlashSize         Total flash size available. When specified, a matching
                      partitions.csv file is written. Only relevant for target
                      ESP32

  --FirmwareSize      (Default: 1MiB) Size of the firmware partition. Set this
                      to a value greater than the reported size by the firmware
                      compiler. If this value is to small, the firmware
                      typically won't boot.

  --ProgramSize       (Default: 1MiB) Size of the partition for the C# code

  -v, --verbose       Output verbose messages.

  -q, --quiet         (Default: false) Minimal output only. This is ignored if
                      -v is specified

  --no-progress       (Default: false) Suppress printing progress messages

  --help              Display this help screen.

  --version           Display version information.
